# Siorb CLI reference

This reference is generated from the compiled Clap command model. The canonical automation surface uses explicit subcommands.

## `siorb`

```text
Explainable cross-platform package orchestration

Usage: siorb [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  install
  remove
  upgrade
  search
  info
  list
  plan
  why
  doctor
  adopt
  reconcile
  repair
  migrate
  bundle
  pin
  unpin
  hold
  unhold
  backend
  source
  catalog
  policy
  audit
  verify
  self
  completion
  version
  help        Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --dry-run


      --json


      --non-interactive


      --yes


      --accept-agreements


      --via <VIA>


      --source <SOURCE>


      --scope <SCOPE>
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: user, system, auto]

      --channel <CHANNEL>
          [default: stable]

      --version <VERSION_CONSTRAINT>


      --arch <ARCH>
          [possible values: x86-64, arm64, x86, arm]

      --offline


      --explain


      --catalog <CATALOG>


      --policy <POLICY>


      --color <COLOR>
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: auto, always, never]

      --verbose...


      --quiet


  -h, --help
          Print help
```
